title: Higher Education vs Vocational Training
passage:
Higher Education and Vocational Training

Higher education is a system that includes universities and colleges. Students go to higher education to learn many subjects. They study for a degree. A degree can help students get good jobs. However, it often takes a long time to finish. Students usually study for three to four years.

Vocational training is different. It is for students who want to learn a specific job. This training is shorter than higher education. Students can learn skills in many areas like cooking, plumbing, or hairdressing. After a short time, they can start working.

Both paths are important. Higher education can give students many options. Vocational training can help students start working faster. Some students choose higher education, while others prefer vocational training. It depends on their goals and interests.

In conclusion, both higher education and vocational training are good choices. Students should think about what they want to do in the future. They can choose the best option for themselves.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students study in higher education?
questions.explanation: Students in higher education study many subjects, as stated in the passage.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does higher education usually take?
questions.explanation: The passage states that higher education often takes three to four years to finish.

questions.text: Vocational training is for students who want to learn a specific job.
Students in vocational training study for many years.
Higher education gives students many options.

questions.instruction: Answer in NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
questions.text: What can vocational training help students do?

vocabulary.meaning: A qualification given to students after they finish higher education.
Relating to training for a specific job.
The ability to do something well.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

Read the passage carefully. Pay attention to the main ideas about higher education and vocational training. Look for specific information to answer the questions. Remember to check if the statements are true, false, or not given in the passage.
